hypotheses.name: Multi-cell-type kNN precision@10 specificity panel
hypotheses.method: For B-cell (n=7), T-cell (n=12), Myeloid (n=3) marker sets: compute precision@k=10 (fraction of kNN from same cell type) at L2, L5, L8, L11. Bootstrap null: 500 random gene sets of matching size. Compare z-scores across cell types.
hypotheses.result_value: B-cell z=4.55, T-cell z=0.29, Myeloid z=1.17
hypotheses.next_action: Expand B-cell marker set further (aim for 15+ in-vocab) and test NK once more markers available. Test at Geneformer embeddings for cross-model validation.

hypotheses.name: Gene-name permutation null for B-cell kNN precision
hypotheses.method: Shuffle gene-to-embedding-row assignment (200 permutations) at L2. Recompute B-cell precision@10 and z-score vs bootstrap null for each permuted dataset. Compare real precision/z to permutation null distribution.
hypotheses.result_value: z=4.38, emp_p=0.005; perm z-scores mean=-0.01 std=1.10
hypotheses.next_action: Apply same permutation null test to T-cell markers to confirm cell-type specificity is also not artifact-driven.

hypotheses.name: B-cell neighborhood functional characterization
hypotheses.method: At L2: for all 7 B-cell markers, collect k=20 nearest neighbors. Count frequency of each gene across B-cell gene neighborhoods. Compute B-cell enrichment ratio vs random expectation. Categorize neighbors biologically (B-cell markers, TFs, immune genes). Compare L2 vs L11.
hypotheses.result_value: Enrichment 2.98x at L2 (0.107 vs 0.036 expected); top neighbors BATF, SPIB, BACH2
hypotheses.next_action: Look up STRING PPI connections between top neighbors and B-cell markers to quantify biological coherence. Also check if SPIB/BACH2 are in the original TRRUST edge dataset.
